content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration · · · Executive/Legal · · · Enforcement · · · Conflict of Interest
(916) 322 5660                                322 5901                322 6441            322 6444


December 13, 1977

77-12-082

Phillip H. Romney
Romney, Smith & Drescher
137 North Tenth Street
Post Office Box 912
Santa Paula, California 93060

Dear Mr. Romney:

     This letter will confirm our telephone conversation of
last week concerning your obligation to disqualify yourself
pursuant to the conflict of interest provisions of the Politi-
cal Reform Act.

     The facts as I understand them are as follows. You are a
private attorney who has contracted with the City of Santa Paula
to act as its City Attorney. The City of Santa Paula is a
member of a joint powers public transportation district. One
of the district's buses was involved in an accident with one
of your private clients. You have asked whether, because of
your position as City Attorney, you are disqualified from
representing your client in a lawsuit against the joint powers
district.

     The Political Reform Act provides in Government Code Section
87100 1/ that no public official shall make, participate in the
making or use his official position to influence a governmental
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a finan-
cial interest. Section 87103 defines financial interest to
include a source of income which may be foreseeably and mater-
ially affected by the decision in question in a manner different
from the effect on the public generally. In the instant case,
the client would be a source of income to you and, therefore,
you are prohibited from making, participating in the making or

_________________________
1/ All statutory references are to the Government Code
unless otherwise indicated.

Phillip H. Romney
December 13, 1977
Page Two


using your official position to influence any governmental
decisions which would foreseeably and materially affect your
client in a manner different from the public generally.

You have indicated to me that you do not anticipate any
involvement in the lawsuit in your capacity as City Attorney.
Moreover, you stated that you understood you would be prohibited
from any such involvement once you have decided to represent
the client in question. Lastly, once the client becomes a
source of income to you, you'll be prohibited, for a period
of twelve months, from making, participating in the making or
using your official position to influence any future governmental decisions which will foreseeably and materially affect
the client in question in a manner different from the public
generally.

With respect to any information on other conflict of
interest laws, you may wish to contact the Attorney General's
Office. If I may be of further assistance, please contact me.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Ted Prim
                                        Chief, Conflicts of
                                        Interest Division

TP:ma

LAW OFFICES
ROMNEY, SMITH & DRESCHER
137 NORTH TENTH STREET
POST OFFICE BOX 912

BLAINE T. ROMNEY
LYMAN R. SMITH
PHILIP D. DRESCHER
PHILLIP H. ROMNEY
W. STEPHEN ONSTOT

SANTA PAULA, CALIFORNIA 93060
(805) 525-7101

November 15, 1977

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Conflict of Interest

Gentlemen:

I am a private attorney on contract with the City of Santa Paula, California, to act as its city attorney.

Currently, I represent a private client against the South Coast Area Transit District, a public entity, formed by a joint powers agreement of which the City of Santa Paula is a signatory.

My question to you is whether or not I am precluded from representing a private individual against the South Coast Area Transit District by virtue of the fact that it was created by a joint powers agreement executed by the City of Santa Paula, an entity which I also represent.

Very truly yours,

ROMNEY, SMITH & DRESCHER

[signature]

Phillip H. Romney

     Currently, I represent a private client against
the South Coast Area Transit District, a public entity,
formed by a joint powers agreement of which the City of

cluded from representing a private individual against the
South Coast Area Transit District by virtue of the fact
that it was created by a joint powers agreement executed
by the City of Santa Paula, an entity which I also repre-
sent.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        ROMNEY, SMITH & DRESCHER

                                        [signed]
                                        Phillip H. Romney

sections.conclusion: You are a city attorney for Santa Paula. One of your clients is a party in a manner different from the public generally. All state law prohibits a source of income to you in the government. You have been told that you do not anticipate any involvement in your capacity as City Attorney. Moreover, you stated that you understood you would be prohibited from any such involvement on which you have decided to represent a client in litigation. Last, once the client becomes a source of income to you, you'll be prohibited, for a period of twelve months, from making, particularly in the making or using your official position to influence any future governmental decisions which will directly and materially affect the entity in question in a manner different from the public generally.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a private attorney serving as city attorney for Santa Paula represent a private client against the South Coast Area Transit District, which was formed by a joint powers agreement with Santa Paula?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Once the private client becomes a source of income, the attorney is prohibited for twelve months from using their city attorney position to influence governmental decisions that directly and materially affect that client differently than the general public. The attorney must also avoid any involvement in the city attorney capacity regarding this representation.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains significant OCR errors and formatting issues making some sections difficult to parse. The conclusion section is partially reconstructed from fragmented OCR text. Response letter is dated December 13, 1977 and signed by Ted Prime. Incoming request letter is dated November 15, 1977 from attorney Phillip H. Romney.

CONCLUSION: You are a city attorney for Santa Paula. One of your clients is a party in a manner different from the public generally. All state law prohibits a source of income to you in the government. You have been told that you do not anticipate any involvement in your capacity as City Attorney. Moreover, you stated that you understood you would be prohibited from any such involvement on which you have decided to represent a client in litigation. Last, once the client becomes a source of income to you, you'll be prohibited, for a period of twelve months, from making, particularly in the making or using your official position to influence any future governmental decisions which will directly and materially affect the entity in question in a manner different from the public generally.

embedding.summary: A Santa Paula city attorney sought FPPC advice on whether representing a private client against the South Coast Area Transit District (formed by joint powers agreement with Santa Paula) creates a conflict of interest. FPPC advised that once the client becomes a source of income, a twelve-month prohibition applies on using the city attorney position to influence governmental decisions affecting that client.
